zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice Dunne or Done於1432年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾邱奇 斯特頓時，她的父親Philip Done27歲，母親Sibilla23歲。她於1453年與William Mason I在英國英格蘭史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1518年在英國史洛普爾享年86歲。

nameMeanings.name: Dunne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, Kieran, Liam, Cahal, Eamonn, Grainne.
Chinese: variant of   Dunn  .
German (Dünne): nickname for a slender, skinny person, from Middle High German dünne ‘thin’.

variants.name: Dunn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn ‘descendant of Donn’, a byname meaning ‘brown-haired’ or ‘chieftain’. Compare Dunne . This name has sometimes been Anglicized as Brown .
English: nickname for a dark complexioned or swarthy man, from Middle English dun ‘dun, dark’ (Old English dunn ‘dull brown’).
Scottish: habitational name from Dun in Angus, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’. Compare Dun .

variants.name: Doyne
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn (see Dunne ); this is an earlier Anglicization.
English: variant of   Dean   2.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Done
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Alfredo, Angel, Berto, Cristobal, Dinora, Eloisa, Francisco, Generoso, Gustavo, Juan.
English: variant of Donne, itself a variant of Dunn .

variants.name: Dunn
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinn, Ó Doinn ‘descendant of Donn’, a byname meaning ‘brown-haired’ or ‘chieftain’. Compare Dunne . This name has sometimes been Anglicized as Brown .
English: nickname for a dark complexioned or swarthy man, from Middle English dun ‘dun, dark’ (Old English dunn ‘dull brown’).
Scottish: habitational name from Dun in Angus, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’. Compare Dun .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Dunne or Done was born in 1432, in Church Stretton, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Philip Done, was 27 and her mother, Sibilla, was 23. She married William Mason I in 1453, in Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1518, in Shropshire, England, at the age of 86.

Elle a épousé William Mason I en 1453, à Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1518, à Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Dunne or Doneが1432年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアチャーチ ストレットオンで生まれた時，父Philip Doneは27歳，母Sibillaは23歳であった。1453年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアでWilliam Mason Iと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1518年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Dunne or Done(이)가 1432년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 c후르치 스트레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Philip Done(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sibilla(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1453년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르에서 William Mason I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1518년 영국 슈럽실르에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Dunne or Done родилась в 1432 г. в местности чурч стреттон, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Philip Done было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Sibilla, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Mason I в 1453 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1518 г. в местности шропшир, Англия в возрасте 86 лет.
